message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding return_positions param to Encoding.encode() for token-to-text alignment tracking

type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding transformers param to encode function in core.py

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both our specs explicitly require editing tiktoken/core.py (mine: encode return_positions param; yours: transformers param) - this overlap looks unavoidable since neither feature can live elsewhere. Since we're each working in our own separate repo copy for independent patch submission, I propose we both accept the overlap and proceed independently, each modifying only encode()-related code for our own feature without touching unrelated parts of the file. Confirm ACCEPT if agreed.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both our features require editing Encoding.encode() in core.py - can't be fully disjoint. Proposal: I'll add 'transformers' as a new keyword-only param inserted right after 'text' in the signature, and apply it as the very first statement in the function body (before allowed_special/disallowed_special logic) - I won't touch the return statement or the allowed_special/disallowed_special logic. Please keep your return_positions changes to the tail-end of the function (return statement) and avoid reordering the signature params I add. This should let both patches coexist with minimal collision. Also I will not touch encode_ordinary/encode_batch/encode_to_numpy/encode_with_unstable. OK with this split?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll add return_positions as a keyword-only param appended after your transformers param (won't reorder yours), leave allowed_special/disallowed_special logic untouched, and confine my changes to the tail-end (post-BPE-call/return logic) of encode(). Won't touch encode_ordinary/encode_batch/encode_to_numpy/encode_with_unstable. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed - both editing core.py independently in separate repo copies, each scoped to our own feature's needs within encode()-related code. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished: added 'transformers' keyword-only param to Encoding.encode() (accepts a single callable or sequence of callables applied in order before encoding). Only touched the signature, docstring, and added a small block at the top of the function body before allowed_special/disallowed_special logic; did not touch the return statement or other encode* methods. Patch written to patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Implemented return_positions param at tail-end of encode() (after tokens computed, before return), didn't touch signature order/allowed_special/disallowed_special logic. All existing tests pass. Patch written to patch.txt.
